# Customer Validation Before an MVP

## Research Question

> Which customer-validation practices are most useful for first-time AI founders before building an MVP?

## Review Status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Owner | FounderOS Editorial |
| Status | review |
| Evidence reviewed as of | 2026-07-13 |
| Sources | 5 |
| Accepted claims | 5 |

## Executive Synthesis

- **synthesis; medium confidence:** Measurement and direct user learning should accompany product experiments rather than relying only on founder intuition. [SRC-003] [SRC-004] [SRC-005]
- **synthesis; medium confidence:** A practical sequence is to select a meaningful problem, state the riskiest assumption, run the smallest measurable test, and build an MVP only when real use is needed for the next evidence. [SRC-001] [SRC-002] [SRC-003] [SRC-004] [SRC-005]

## Method

FounderOS validated structured source, evidence, and claim records. Source advice is labeled separately from synthesis and assumptions. This brief does not treat practitioner guidance as controlled experimental evidence.

## Findings

- **source-opinion; medium confidence:** An MVP should test demand for one clear product function with real users rather than maximize its feature count. [SRC-001]
- **source-opinion; medium confidence:** First-time founders can reduce premature implementation by launching a stripped-down service and tolerating manual work initially. [SRC-002]
- **source-opinion; medium confidence:** Validation experiments are easier to interpret when the hypothesis, test, measurement, and success threshold are recorded before execution. [SRC-005]
- **synthesis; medium confidence:** Measurement and direct user learning should accompany product experiments rather than relying only on founder intuition. [SRC-003] [SRC-004] [SRC-005]
- **synthesis; medium confidence:** A practical sequence is to select a meaningful problem, state the riskiest assumption, run the smallest measurable test, and build an MVP only when real use is needed for the next evidence. [SRC-001] [SRC-002] [SRC-003] [SRC-004] [SRC-005]

## Conflicting Evidence

No explicit claim conflicts are recorded.

## Limitations

- This is practitioner guidance, not a controlled comparison of MVP designs.
- The recommendation may not apply unchanged to regulated or safety-critical products.
- A well-structured test can still target the wrong assumption or audience.
- The sources provide practitioner frameworks rather than quantified causal evidence.
- The sequence is FounderOS synthesis and has not been tested as a universal process.
- AI products may require additional evaluation for model reliability and safety.

## Open Assumptions

- **CLM-006:** The same validation sequence works without modification for every category of AI product.
